arts.ttl: Olmo in limbo as La Liga and Spanish FA rule against Barca
arts.sum: Barcelona midfielder Dani Olmo and forward Pau Victor have been blocked from playing in the rest of the 2024-25 season by Spain's football authorities.

After it was reported in the last couple of days that Girona were looking to sign Eric Garcia, a second Barcelona player has now appear on their radar. On Friday, Barcelona made their long-awaited return to La Liga’s 1:1 rule, and on the back of this, they could sanction the sale of Eric Garcia in […] The post Girona eye double Barcelona deal appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Barcelona plot final attempt to re-register Dani Olmo after official La Liga ruling
arts.sum: Barcelona's plea to re-register Dani Olmo was officially rejected but the club remain "optimistic" a resolution will be found.

arts.ttl: Barcelona uncertainty goes on as latest request to register Dani Olmo is rejected
arts.sum: La Liga and RFEF reject Olmo and Víctor registrations Barcelona likely to take case to Spain’s sports council Barcelona have failed in their latest attempt to register the Spain international Dani Olmo for the second half of the season. On Saturday, La Liga and the Spanish football federation (RFEF) jointly rejected the registrations of Olmo and his teammate Pau Víctor. Olmo, 26, joined Barcelona from RB Leipzig in August for a reported €60m (£51m). They could only register the attacking player for the initial stage of the campaign, though, because of difficulties meeting La Liga’s wage restrictions. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Two Barcelona stars to be handed first starts of the season vs Barbastro
arts.sum: Barcelona head coach Hansi Flick is expected to afford a first starting berth of the campaign to not one, but two members of his squad later this evening. Barcelona, of…

arts.ttl: 'Olmo crisis leaves Barcelona again mired in uncertainty'
arts.sum: With Barcelona unable to register summer signing Dani Olmo for the second half of the season, Spanish football expert Guillem Balague looks at the club's current crisis.

arts.ttl: Dani Olmo registration saga: What next for Barcelona?
arts.sum: Barcelona will reportedly file a complaint to the Spanish government after seeing their latest bid to register Dani Olmo and Pau Victor rejected by the RFEF. All of which means Olmo and Victor must now wait even longer to find out what happens next and if they can play for the club for the rest of the season.

arts.ttl: Why Barcelona can't play Dani Olmo or Pau Victor ... for now
arts.sum: Though the stadium is not yet operational, as it is being renovated ahead of a partial re-opening later this year, Barça had hoped the money raised would serve to register star signing Dani Olmo and young forward Pau Víctor with LaLiga for the second half of the season. The club's final option was the sale of VIP boxes at Camp Nou. Sources have told ESPN the deal is worth around €100m, but finalising the terms, getting the paperwork to LaLiga and providing proof of the money before the Dec. 31 deadline was difficult.

arts.ttl: Barcelona learn Dani Olmo fate as LaLiga issue final decision on player registration
arts.sum: Barcelona have tried every trick in the book to get Dani Olmo registered as one of their players, but LaLiga have knocked them back in what could be the final blow. The Catalan outfit signed the attacking midfielder for €55 million from RB Leipzig last summer, but were unable to initially register him due to […] The post Barcelona learn Dani Olmo fate as LaLiga issue final decision on player registration first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Spanish league again denies Barcelona's request to register Olmo
arts.sum: The league and the federation said in a joint statement that even though Barcelona eventually was able to meet the salary-cap rules, the players can’t be registered again after already having been dropped because of the missed deadline. Each Spanish league club has a salary cap that is calculated based on a series of factors, including revenues, costs and debts.
